title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: You or someone you care about may have been diagnosed with a blood cancer.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: This video will help you understand how it can be managed.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Blood cancer, also known as hematologic cancer, begins in blood-forming tissue such as bone marrow.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Bone marrow is the spongy tissue inside your bones that makes blood cells,

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: or blood cancer may start within the cells of the immune system.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Examples of blood cancer are leukemia, lymphoma, and multiple myeloma.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Treatments such as chemotherapy, radiation therapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy,

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: and stem cell transplant may control blood cancers depending on the type of cancer and other factors.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: It is important to have a care plan in place not only for receiving therapy and personal care, but also for follow-up care.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: This includes scheduling and going to follow-up appointments.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: In addition to checking your general health and well-being, your doctor will want to check for any signs of the cancer returning

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: and check for health problems that may have resulted from treatment.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: For example, you may have tests and imaging studies performed, including taking samples of your blood,

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: fluid around your spinal cord, or bone marrow to check for cancer as relevant for your situation.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Continue taking all your medications as prescribed.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Do not stop taking them without talking to your doctor.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: It is helpful to take notes for points to discuss that your next doctor's visit.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: This could include any side effects you may have noticed.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: If you've had treatments such as chemotherapy, radiation therapy, or a bone marrow transplant,

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: follow any instructions you were given.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: There are lifestyle changes that can improve your health and help you feel better.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: There are benefits to stopping smoking.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: This lowers the risk for cancer returning or spreading.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: It's generally accepted that eating a healthy diet can help you feel better and stronger,

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: both during and after treatment.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Physical activities like walking, riding a bicycle or swimming can help you feel better and less tired.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Talk to your doctor before starting an exercise routine.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Knowing you have cancer can be overwhelming.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: You may have worries about your condition and how sick you could get,

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: how it will affect you and your family, treatments and hospital stays, medical bills, and your job.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: There are ways to find support for you.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Remember that your doctor and healthcare team are there to answer any questions you have.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: The following sources of support can help you, social workers, faith leaders, counselors, and support groups.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: Talk to your doctor if you have any questions about your treatment plan, medications, or lifestyle changes

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
text_en: to help you manage your blood cancer.
